task.objective: Enhance the `get_trends` MCP tool to support flexible time granularity beyond fixed day intervals. Add support for hourly, weekly, and monthly aggregation, plus explicit date range filtering.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - MCP Server Tools
context.background:
The `get_trends` tool provides time-series data for pass/fail rates but only supports a `days` parameter (1-90). This is limiting for:
- Hourly monitoring: Detecting issues that happened in the last few hours - Weekly/monthly reports: Aggregating data for periodic reviews - Custom date ranges: Analyzing specific periods (e.g., sprint boundaries)
Common use cases requiring flexibility: - "Show me test trends for the last 4 weeks" - "What was the pass rate hour-by-hour yesterday?" - "Compare January vs February test health"
context.audience: - AI assistants generating trend reports - Developers analyzing test health patterns - QA teams preparing periodic health summaries
context.impact: MEDIUM - Enables more flexible trend analysis and reporting. Particularly useful for sprint retrospectives and incident analysis.

issue.fix: Add period parameter for hour/day/week/month granularity

issue.description: No explicit date range support in MCP tool
issue.actual: DB function supports dateRange but tool doesn't expose it
issue.fix: Add from/to parameters to tool schema

functional.requirement: Add period parameter with hour/day/week/month options
Add from/to date parameters for explicit ranges
Rename count parameter to replace "days" with generic "count"
Maintain backwards compatibility with days parameter
Add proper pass_rate calculation in response

non_functional.requirement: Hourly queries should be limited to 7 days max
Monthly queries should be limited to 2 years max

solution.approach: 1. Add period enum parameter (hour, day, week, month) 2. Add from/to parameters with proper date parsing 3. Update database function to use DATE_TRUNC for different periods 4. Keep days parameter as fallback for backwards compatibility
solution.types:
type TrendPeriod = 'hour' | 'day' | 'week' | 'month'
interface TrendDataPoint { period: string // ISO date or datetime depending on granularity executions: number // Renamed from "total" passed: number failed: number skipped: number pass_rate: number // 0-100 }

steps.step: 1. Start dev server: npm run dev
2. Call get_trends with no parameters - verify daily data returned
3. Call with period="hour" count=24 - verify hourly data
4. Call with period="week" count=4 - verify weekly aggregation
5. Call with period="month" count=3 - verify monthly aggregation
6. Call with explicit from/to range - verify date filtering
7. Verify backwards compatibility with days=7 parameter

test.script:
# Test 1: Default behavior (should work as before) curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_trends", "args": {}}'
# Test 2: Hourly granularity curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_trends", "args": {"period": "hour", "count": 24}}'
# Test 3: Weekly aggregation curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_trends", "args": {"period": "week", "count": 4}}'
# Test 4: Explicit date range curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_trends", "args": {"from": "2026-01-01", "to": "2026-01-05"}}'
# Test 5: Backwards compatibility curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/mcp/tools \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $MCP_TOKEN" \ -d '{"tool": "get_trends", "args": {"days": 14}}'

implementation_order.step: Create TrendOptions interface and TrendDataPoint type
Update getTrendData function signature and implementation
Manual testing with various period/count combinations

always.rule: Default to period="day" for backwards compatibility
Validate count limits per period type
Return pass_rate as 0-100 number in response
Sort results by period ascending (oldest first)

never.rule: Never allow hourly queries beyond 7 days (168 hours)
Never allow monthly queries beyond 2 years (24 months)
Never break existing days parameter behavior

documentation_updates.description: The MCP documentation page must be updated to reflect the new parameters (period, count, from, to) and updated response fields for get_trends.

update.current_code: { name: "get_trends", description: "Get time-series trend data for pass/fail rates. Useful for charts and tracking health over time.", category: "analysis" as const, parameters: [ { name: "days", type: "number", default: "7", description: "Days to look back (1-90)" }, ], responseFields: [ "date: ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD)", "executions: number - Runs on this day", "passed: number - Passed tests", "failed: number - Failed tests", "pass_rate: number (0-100)", ], },
update.updated_code: { name: "get_trends", description: "Get time-series trend data with flexible granularity. Supports hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly aggregation.", category: "analysis" as const, parameters: [ { name: "period", type: "string", default: "day", description: "Granularity: hour | day | week | month" }, { name: "count", type: "number", description: "Number of periods to look back" }, { name: "days", type: "number", description: "DEPRECATED: Use count + period instead" }, { name: "from", type: "string", description: "Start date (ISO 8601)" }, { name: "to", type: "string", description: "End date (ISO 8601)" }, ], responseFields: [ "period: ISO datetime - Period start", "executions: number - Total runs", "passed: number - Passed count", "failed: number - Failed count", "skipped: number - Skipped count", "pass_rate: number (0-100)", ], example: `"Show me weekly test trends for the last month"`, },

update.note: Update the /api/trends endpoint documentation to include the new period, count, from, to parameters if the API also exposes these.

files_to_modify.file: lib/db.ts - Update getTrendData function with period support
lib/mcp/tools.ts - Update tool definition and handler

output_format.complexity: Medium - Requires DATE_TRUNC and validation logic
